scenario: Beacon Healthcare runs Camunda 8.8 across 6 clusters and processes 9500 claims per day. An operator plans to delete an older process definition version from the Processes page in Operate. That version contains four user tasks, of which 320 instances are currently open in Tasklist for nurses to complete.
question: What happens in Tasklist to the user tasks of that process definition when the operator deletes it?

options.text: The user tasks of that process definition are kept in Tasklist as read-only items so assignees can still see their history
The user tasks of that process definition are reassigned in Tasklist to the next higher version once the deletion completes
The user tasks of that process definition are deleted from Tasklist as part of the delete operation on the Processes page
The user tasks of that process definition are kept in Tasklist until the assigned worker manually claims and completes each one

a.text: Incorrect. The docs state that if a process definition contains user tasks, they are deleted from Tasklist; they are not retained in Tasklist as a read-only history for assignees to browse.

b.text: Incorrect. The delete flow does not perform a migration; there is no mention in the docs of reassigning user tasks to a newer version of the definition before or after deletion.

c.text: Correct. The Processes page impact note states that when a deleted process definition contains user tasks, those tasks are deleted from Tasklist as a documented side effect of the delete operation.

d.text: Incorrect. The user tasks linked to the deleted definition are removed from Tasklist as part of the delete; they are not left waiting for the assigned worker to claim and complete them.

explanation: When a deleted process definition contains user tasks, those user tasks are removed from Tasklist as part of the deletion impact described on the Processes page section of the delete-resources docs.
